title: rosa coriifolia var. agrestis
intro: Rosa coriifolia var. agrestis, also known as wild rose or dog rose, is a perennial shrub native to Europe and parts of Asia, characterized by its thorny stems and fragrant, white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and combat infections. The health benefits of Rosa coriifolia var. agrestis include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and supporting digestive health, making it a popular herbal remedy for a range of ailments. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or topical salve, allowing for various forms of herbal use, including internal consumption and external application.
